reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing immutable conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Generation runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (models/deepseek-r1-webllm/worker.js), which imports lib/webllm.js and calls streamChat() → engine.chat.completions.create({ ...req, stream: true }) on the MLC WebLLM engine created for build "DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B-q4f16_1-MLC" (from deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B). Tokens are streamed back as real deltas; there is no canned path. Conformance calls-real-inference (source, matches CreateMLCEngine/createModelLoader) and loads-clean (console-clean) are tested pass; io-shape-semantic is a manual-evidenced assertion.
rubric.notes: WebLLM is WebGPU-ONLY (no WASM fallback); the page gates on a real GPU adapter via webGPUAdapterAvailable() before ever asking the worker to load. I did not run a fresh live inference in this backfill, so no measured latency/token numbers are claimed.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = an OpenAI-shaped streaming chat completion; the page reads chunk.choices[0].delta.content, appends each delta to the assistant bubble, and shows the final text. The exact messages array sent (system + history + user) is rendered in the 'See inside' wire panel, and real engine.runtimeStatsText() tok/s is shown when exposed.
rubric.notes: See-inside splits prefill (time-to-first-token) vs decode from the real streamed timing (performance.now() in the worker); generated count is honestly labelled as streamed chunks (≈ tokens), context as a ~4-chars/token estimate.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact source model id (deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B, linked to its card), the MLC build id (DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B-q4f16_1-MLC), runtime = WebLLM/MLC (explicitly NOT transformers.js), task = text-generation, params/dtype (q4f16_1), backend = WebGPU-only, download ≈4500 MB (cached after first load), context window, and license (MIT). Conformance declares-model-id/declares-task/declares-quantisation (q4f16) + declares-backend all tested pass.
rubric.notes: The worker's MODEL_ID constant matches the at-a-glance MLC build id — no invented model or backend. WebLLM weights are downloaded+compiled by MLC into WebGPU shaders.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (MODEL = { modelId: "DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B-q4f16_1-MLC", runtime: "webllm", dtype: "q4f16_1", sizeMB: 4500, requiresWebGPU: true }); conformance shared-auto-init-loader (source, createModelLoader) tested pass. A valid current on-device model auto-initialises with no Load click; no-adapter surfaces the honest needs-WebGPU state instead of a fake reply.
rubric.notes: modelId is keyed on the MLC build id so the cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee (auto-init-on-cached is a manual-evidenced assertion).

rubric.evidence: Loading/progress/ready/error is announced in role=status aria-live (conformance live-status-region pass). Failures surface on the page ('Generation failed: ' + message on status.err), not just console (errors-on-the-page source pass). On a device with no GPU adapter the page shows a labelled needs-WebGPU state (webgpuFallbackHTML) with the real reason, chrome://gpu / enable-unsafe-webgpu steps, and the ≈4500 MB cost — webgpu-capability-honest (probe) is in the suite. A cooperative Stop button calls engine.interruptGenerate().
rubric.notes: WebGPU-only + ~4.5 GB (8B): high-VRAM desktop only; on phones and low-VRAM GPUs this degrades to the honest needs-WebGPU/too-large state, never a fake reply.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the system-prompt textarea, temperature/top_p/max-tokens range sliders (values reflect live via input listeners), the message composer textarea (Enter to send, Shift+Enter newline), Send, Stop (hidden until generating), and Clear conversation. The ladder adds basics/practical/wild/multi-model pages driving the same engine.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is a manual-evidenced conformance assertion; the sliders feed the real generation options passed to the worker.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the assistant bubble is filled purely from streamed WebLLM deltas; the runtime stats come from engine.runtimeStatsText(). When WebGPU is unavailable the page shows the honest needs-WebGPU explanation, never a fabricated reply. no-fake-output is a manual-evidenced assertion in the suite.

Conformance buttons-have-names + images-have-alt (script) and live-status-region (dom, [role=status][aria-live]) tested pass at build. The status line is role=status aria-live=polite; the chat log is aria-live. HOWEVER the responsive pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets pending agent verification (support=needs-review, automatedNotes: 10 on desktop / 4 on mobile — mostly the range-slider thumbs and small secondary buttons), below this repo's ~44px tap-target bar.
rubric.notes: Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system (public/styles.css). The sub-24px flag is the real open a11y item on this demo.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses fluid flex/grid: controls flex-wrap, the chat/transcript log is intrinsically sized (min/max-block-size + overflow-y), textareas/canvas use inline-size, and the see-inside stat grid is auto-fit minmax. Conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are in the immutable suite. support=needs-review on both classes: the automated pre-scan reported 10 sub-24px target(s) desktop / 4 mobile awaiting an agent matrix pass — no live matrix was run in this backfill. This build is ≈4500 MB; at ~4.5 GB it is desktop/high-VRAM only — it will not fit on mainstream mobile.
rubric.notes: I did NOT run responsive-check in this retrospective pass, so I do not claim a measured 0px-overflow result; the honest status is needs-review with the sub-24px tap-target flag.

rubric.evidence: All decoding runs in the Web Worker via the MLC WebGPU engine; the main thread only appends streamed deltas and updates a blinking caret (guarded by prefers-reduced-motion) — no synchronous inference or unbounded loop on the UI thread. Conformance inference-off-main-thread (source, new Worker/worker.js) tested pass; inp-stays-low is a manual-evidenced assertion.
rubric.notes: The composer textareas use field-sizing:content so they grow/shrink without JS reflow work.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread web worker streaming tokens
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive (INP): move heavy work off the main thread; avoid long blocking tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all token decoding runs in a dedicated module Worker driving MLC's WebGPU engine; the UI thread only appends streamed deltas, keeping INP low.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread streamChat; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks main thread responsiveness
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up heavy synchronous processing so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generation is streamed token-by-token from the worker rather than a single blocking main-thread call; the page never runs a long synchronous loop.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: streamChat async iterator posts per-delta messages; no main-thread generate()

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keyboard operability.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — load/progress/ready/error announced via role=status aria-live; every button carries an accessible name (buttons-have-names pass). EXCEPTION pending: sub-24px slider/secondary-button tap targets flagged for an agent matrix pass.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region + buttons-have-names pass; support=needs-review sub-24px note

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — controls flex-wrap, the chat log is intrinsically sized with overflow-y, textareas use inline-size:100%, and the stat grid is auto-fit minmax — no fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: CHAT_CSS flex-wrap + min/max-block-size + auto-fit grid; responsive-desktop/mobile assertions in suite

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Let form fields grow/shrink to fit input with field-sizing plus min/max limits for a responsive composer.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the composer + system-prompt textareas use field-sizing:content with min/max-block-size so they auto-fit the message without manual JS resizing.

openQuestions: Verify and enlarge the 10 desktop / 4 mobile sub-24px tap targets (mainly range-slider thumbs and secondary buttons) to the ~44px bar, then flip support from needs-review to ok with a real mobile+desktop matrix pass.
The ~4.5 GB 8B build is desktop/high-VRAM only — mobile should almost certainly be recorded too-large-for-this-device; confirm the desktop VRAM floor too.

The MLC engine is dynamic-imported from the esm.run CDN (https://esm.run/@mlc-ai/web-llm) at runtime — worth confirming first-load resilience/offline behaviour and whether a pinned/self-hosted build is warranted.

followUpGoals.goal: Run the responsive matrix (scripts/responsive-check.mjs) at 360×740 and 1280×800, enlarge the flagged sub-24px tap targets, and record support=ok/unsupported per class.
Record mobile (and low-VRAM desktop) as too-large-for-this-device for the ~4.5 GB DeepSeek-R1-8B build with evidence, and confirm the minimum VRAM that actually runs it.

summary: Revision-1 retrospective critique backfill for deepseek-r1-webllm (deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B → MLC build DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B-q4f16_1-MLC, text-generation, WebLLM/MLC, q4f16_1, WebGPU-ONLY, ≈4500 MB, MIT). Evidence grounded in code inspection + the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, shared-auto-init-loader, inference-off-main-thread, declares-* , live-status-region, buttons-have-names all tested pass at build). Real streaming chat via lib/webllm.js streamChat in a dedicated worker; honest needs-WebGPU degrade (no WASM fallback), never a faked reply; see-inside splits real prefill/decode timing + engine tok/s. This is an 8B REASONING (R1-distill) model that streams a chain-of-thought trace before its answer; the worker takes an optional modelId per message so the multi-model page can spin a second model on the same code path. GENUINE OPEN ITEM: support=needs-review with 10/4 sub-24px tap targets pending an agent matrix pass; no fresh live-inference numbers are claimed in this backfill.
